message:
fix(backup): handle table query failures gracefully in SQL dump

Replace unwrap() with match in dump_database_sql() when querying table
rows. If a table query fails (e.g. locked by concurrent writer, corrupt
table), log a warning and continue to the next table instead of panicking
the entire backup process.

Also syncs beads issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-og6q6.3","title":"[replay][T2] Deterministic Replay Execution Kernel","description":"Build the offline replay runtime that consumes .ftreplay artifacts and reproduces baseline decisions and effects deterministically via virtual time, stable ordering, and controlled adapters for nondeterministic boundaries.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Replay runtime executes artifacts without live side effects.
2. Virtual clock and scheduler enforce deterministic progression.
3. Event merge and ordering rules are explicit and reproducible.
4. Provenance logs explain why each replay decision occurred.
5. Replay can checkpoint and resume with consistent outcomes.
6. Track includes determinism unit and property tests plus e2e replay harnesses with detailed structured logs.","notes":"This is the technical heart of the initiative. Any nondeterminism introduced here invalidates downstream diff and gate logic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:45:28.589624Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:03:07.392743Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","deterministic-replay","engine","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2841,"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Treat deterministic execution as a product requirement, not an implementation detail. If replay is flaky, trust in the whole system collapses.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:45:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-og6q6.3.1","title":"[replay][T2.1] Implement virtual clock and deterministic scheduler core","description":"Create a virtual-time execution model and deterministic scheduler that replaces wall-clock variability during replay. Ensure every replay run follows the same scheduling semantics under identical artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Virtual clock abstraction implemented for replay runtime.
2. Scheduler decisions are deterministic and repeatable.
3. Wall-clock dependencies are isolated behind adapters.
4. Tests prove repeated runs produce identical schedule traces.
5. Verification requirement: includes task-scoped unit, integration, and property tests where applicable, at least one end-to-end harness script, and structured JSON logging with deterministic run_id, step, status, duration, and error context fields.
6. e2e harness requirement: at least one realistic e2e script per task path must run in CI and emit structured JSON logs with run_id, scenario_id where relevant, timing, assertions, and failure context.","notes":"Clock/scheduling nondeterminism is the most common replay invalidation vector.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:08.077439Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:24:26.204662Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic-replay","runtime","scheduler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2842,"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Design principle: time is input data during replay, not ambient environment.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-og6q6.3.2","title":"[replay][T2.2] Build stable event ordering and pane merge resolver","description":"Implement canonical ordering rules for multi-pane/multi-stream events, including tie-breaker semantics and merge behavior for concurrent records, so replay input order is unambiguous and reproducible.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Canonical ordering rules documented and implemented.
2. Tie-breakers are deterministic and test-covered.
3. Merge behavior for concurrent events is stable across runs.
4. Ordering anomalies produce explicit diagnostics.
5. Verification requirement: includes task-scoped unit, integration, and property tests where applicable, at least one end-to-end harness script, and structured JSON logging with deterministic run_id, step, status, duration, and error context fields.
6. e2e harness requirement: at least one realistic e2e script per task path must run in CI and emit structured JSON logs with run_id, scenario_id where relevant, timing, assertions, and failure context.","notes":"Ordering ambiguity leads to spurious diffs and trust erosion.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:08.856369Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:24:26.559499Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic-replay","events","ordering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2843,"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Future-self: hidden tie-breakers are technical debt; make every ordering rule explicit and test-visible.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:09Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-og6q6.3.3","title":"[replay][T2.3] Implement side-effect isolation adapters for replay runtime","description":"Provide adapter layer that prevents live side effects during replay (I/O, sends, external calls) while still preserving semantic outcomes needed for decision evaluation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Replay path cannot trigger live mutating actions.\n2. Adapter stubs preserve enough semantics for policy/workflow evaluation.\n3. Unsupported side-effect domains fail closed with clear errors.\n4. Safety tests prove side-effect isolation.\n2. Verification requirement: includes task-scoped unit, integration, and property tests where applicable, at least one end-to-end harness script, and structured JSON logging with deterministic run_id, step, status, duration, and error context fields.\n2. e2e harness requirement: at least one realistic e2e script per task path must run in CI and emit structured JSON logs with run_id, scenario_id where relevant, timing, assertions, and failure context.","notes":"Replay must be safe-by-default even under malformed artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:09.628969Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:23:13.853388Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adapters","deterministic-replay","runtime","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2844,"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Critical safety property: replay is analysis-only unless an explicit future mode intentionally opts into controlled execution.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:10Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-og6q6.3.4","title":"[replay][T2.4] Emit replay provenance logs and decision-explanation trace","description":"Emit structured provenance for each replay step: input record, derived state transition, decision rationale, and resulting effect, so diff findings are explainable and auditable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every decision/effect has provenance entry.\n2. Logs include causal pointers to source artifact records.\n3. Provenance output is queryable and machine-consumable.\n4. Diagnostic views help isolate divergence root causes quickly.\n2. -{"id":"ft-og6q6.3.5","title":"[replay][T2.5] Add replay checkpoint/resume and deterministic failure semantics","description":"Support replay checkpointing and deterministic resume behavior so long runs and partial failures can be recovered without changing outcomes or introducing hidden drift.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Replay can checkpoint and resume with equivalence guarantees.\n2. Failure semantics are deterministic and documented.\n3. Resume path preserves ordering and state invariants.\n4. Recovery tests cover interruption and restart scenarios.\n2. Verification requirement: includes task-scoped unit, integration, and property tests where applicable, at least one end-to-end harness script, and structured JSON logging with deterministic run_id, step, status, duration, and error context fields.\n2. e2e harness requirement: at least one realistic e2e script per task path must run in CI and emit structured JSON logs with run_id, scenario_id where relevant, timing, assertions, and failure context.","notes":"Operational replay workflows need resumability for large artifacts and CI reliability.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:11.526953Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:23:14.650390Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic-replay","reliability","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2846,"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Resumability must preserve semantic identity, not just progress markers.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:12Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","title":"[replay][T2.6] Build kernel determinism unit/property/e2e suite with trace logging","description":"Implement a full determinism proof suite for replay runtime: unit tests for scheduler and ordering rules, property tests for replay invariants under randomized input permutations, and e2e replay scripts with detailed provenance logs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests validate clock, scheduler, ordering, and side-effect isolation behavior.
2. Property tests prove invariant preservation across repeated and permuted inputs.
3. E2E replay scripts execute baseline runs repeatedly and assert byte-stable outputs where required.
4. Trace logs include causal chain pointers to artifact record IDs.
5. Failure output provides minimal reproducible case extraction.","notes":"This task is the trust anchor for deterministic execution claims.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:03:11.271902Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:04:30.345474Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic-replay","e2e","logging","property-tests","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2847,"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.3.6","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Determinism evidence must be statistically and operationally convincing, not anecdotal;  {"id":"ft-og6q6.4.1","title":"[replay][T3.1] Define counterfactual override package format and loader","description":"Define package format for candidate overrides (rules, policy config, workflow config) and implement loader with strict validation so scenario runs are reproducible and auditable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Override package schema/versioning implemented.\n2. Loader validates package consistency and compatibility.\n3. Applied override set is recorded in replay metadata.\n4. Invalid overrides fail fast with actionable diagnostics.\n2. Verification requirement: includes task-scoped unit, integration, and property tests where applicable, at least one end-to-end harness script, and structured JSON logging with deterministic run_id, step, status, duration, and error context fields.\n2. e2e harness requirement: at least one realistic e2e script per task path must run in CI and emit structured JSON logs with run_id, scenario_id where relevant, timing, assertions, and failure context.","notes":"Scenario reproducibility requires immutable, declared override inputs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:13.697489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:23:15.025649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["counterfactual","policy","rules","schema","workflow"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2849,"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.4.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Treat override bundles as experiment manifests, not ad hoc runtime flags.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-og6q6.4.2","title":"[replay][T3.2] Implement fault-injection DSL for high-value failure modes","description":"Create a declarative DSL for injecting common operational failures (timeouts, dropped events, lag spikes, policy backend errors) against captured runs to stress candidate behavior safely.","acceptance_criteria":"1. DSL supports key failure classes relevant to ft workflows.\n2. Injection points are deterministic and reproducible.\n3. Fault runs emit clear annotations in provenance/diff outputs.\n4. Invalid fault specs are validated pre-run.\n2. Verification requirement: includes task-scoped unit, integration, and property tests where applicable, at least one end-to-end harness script, and structured JSON logging with deterministic run_id, step, status, duration, and error context fields.\n2. e2e harness requirement: at least one realistic e2e script per task path must run in CI and emit structured JSON logs with run_id, scenario_id where relevant, timing, assertions, and failure context.","notes":"Fault injection should expand confidence surface, not create random chaos.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:14.506895Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:23:15.427046Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["counterfactual","fault-injection","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-og6q6.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:21:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2850,"issue_id":"ft-og6q6.4.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Keep injection model explicit and deterministic; random fuzzing belongs to separate stress tracks.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-og6q6.4.3","title":"[replay][T3.3] Build scenario matrix runner for baseline-vs-candidate sweeps","description":"Implement matrix runner that executes many scenario combinations (artifact x override package x fault profile) and aggregates outputs into indexed result sets for downstream diff scoring.","acceptance_criteria":"1.
